name: Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop
name_original: Navas del Madroño desde Brozas – ida y vuelta por los embalses
description: Cycle a moderate 15.8-mile route around the reservoirs from Brozas to Navas del Madroño, enjoying Extremadura's green landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Brozas traverse the green landscapes of Extremadura, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. The region features open plains, pasture lands, and several reservoirs, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Gentle slopes characterize much of the area, with some routes incorporating sections through natural parks. Best touring cycling routes around Brozas The most popular touring cycling route is Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop, a 16.9 miles (27.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores parts of the Tagus International Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop, a moderate 15.8 miles (25.4 km) path. This route winds around local reservoirs, offering tranquil views across the water. Local touring cyclists also love the Puente río Casillas loop from Arroyo de la Luz, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Brozas is defined by green landscapes, plains and pasture lands, and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Brozas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Brozas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Brozas?
A: The touring cycling routes around Brozas offer a varied landscape. You'll find yourself traversing the green landscapes of Extremadura, with open plains, pasture lands, and several tranquil reservoirs. While many routes feature mostly paved surfaces, some sections, particularly those exploring natural parks, may include unpaved paths. The area is generally characterized by gentle slopes, making for a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes for different fitness levels in Brozas?
A: Yes, Brozas offers touring cycling routes suitable for various fitness levels. Out of 17 available routes, you'll find 3 easy routes perfect for a relaxed ride, 13 moderate routes for those with good fitness, and 1 difficult route for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. This variety ensures there's something for everyone.

Q: What natural features can I explore while touring cycling near Brozas?
A: The region around Brozas is rich in natural beauty. Many routes wind around local reservoirs, offering tranquil views. You can also explore parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus International Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its rivers and historical sites. The Llanos de Brozas, characterized by plains and pasture lands, are a haven for birdwatching, with opportunities to spot diverse bird species. Additionally, the Charca de Brozas Periurban Conservation and Leisure Park is a protected natural area close to the town.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Brozas and its surroundings are steeped in history. You can cycle through the historic center of Brozas, which boasts magnificent fortresses and interesting noble buildings like the palace of Argüillos. Routes often incorporate historical sites, such as those found within the Tagus International Natural Park. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;town of Brozas&lt;/a&gt; itself, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Brozas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5576763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Stone Bridge over the Salor River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Brozas?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Brozas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114110581/brozas-and-navas-del-madrono-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas and Navas del Madroño Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.8-mile path that winds around local reservoirs. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Brozas – Main Square of Brozas loop from Brozas&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the town's historical areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Brozas?
A: The Extremadura region, where Brozas is located, is known for its lush and verdant scenery, especially during certain seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with the landscapes at their most picturesque. Winters can also be suitable, particularly for birdwatching in areas like the Llanos de Brozas, where species like the Common Crane can be observed.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Brozas offers easy touring cycling routes that are great for beginners or families looking for a relaxed ride. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17317505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente río Casillas loop from Arroyo de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.1-mile trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. These routes typically feature gentle slopes and less challenging terrain.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Brozas typically take to complete?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Brozas varies depending on their length and difficulty. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.9 miles (27.2 km), typically takes around 1 hour 44 minutes. Longer routes, like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aliseda – Aceitunilla Pond loop from Aliseda&lt;/a&gt; (37.2 miles / 59.8 km), can take over 5 hours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Brozas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Brozas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's green landscapes, the peaceful atmosphere around the reservoirs, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest. The variety of routes catering to different abilities is also frequently appreciated.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there is at least one difficult touring cycling route in the Brozas area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aliseda – Aceitunilla Pond loop from Aliseda&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 37.2-mile (59.8 km) route with significant elevation gain, making it suitable for those with high fitness levels looking for an extended adventure.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural scenery with cultural exploration?
A: Yes, many routes in Brozas offer a fantastic combination of natural scenery and cultural exploration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1114096652/alcantara-and-villa-del-rey-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara and Villa del Rey Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Tagus International Natural Park while also passing by historical sites. Similarly, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Brozas – Main Square of Brozas loop from Brozas&lt;/a&gt; allows you to immerse yourself in the town's rich heritage amidst the surrounding landscapes.
tips: Brozas is an important historical town on the trade route between Cáseres and Portugal and was founded by the Order of Alcántara in the 13th century. Today you can see the magnificent castle, the Palacio de los Gutiérrez-Flores, the heritage that surrounds the town square, the market and the churches and the Convento de los Remedios as monuments to the wealth of its past.

Brozas Castle, also known as “Castillo de la Comienda Mayor de la Orden de Alcántara” and popularly called “the Palace”, is a fortification located in the Spanish town of Brozas, in the province of Cáceres. Its history dates back to the 14th century, although its current appearance comes from a reconstruction carried out in the 16th-17th centuries.

The landscape between Brozas and Navas del Madroño is dotted with small reservoirs that collect rainwater for irrigation and drinking. These become rich wetlands that support birds on their migratory routes. Many gravel trails in the areas run between these reservoirs.

Navas del Madroño is a small rural town located at the crossroads of three cattle ravines that lead to the capital Cáceres between the Alcántara and Alconétar bridges. Today, when you cycle to the city, don't forget to visit its most characteristic monuments, the Church of Nuestra Señora de la O, and stop for a coffee in the Plaza de Constitución.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Navas del Madroño from Brozas cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this route are not detailed, you can typically find parking in the towns of Brozas or Navas del Madroño, which serve as the start and end points for this circular tour. Look for public parking spaces within these towns.

faqs.question: Is the Navas del Madroño from Brozas cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a general rule for cycling routes in natural areas, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for this route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Navas del Madroño from Brozas route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region, known for its green areas and forests, is particularly beautiful in spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, allowing you to fully enjoy the scenic reservoirs and natural surroundings. Summer can be very hot, and winter might be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, typically, public cycling routes like the Navas del Madroño from Brozas – round trip through the reservoirs do not require special permits or charge entrance fees. You are free to enjoy the natural landscape and reservoirs without additional costs.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Navas del Madroño from Brozas cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved tracks, making for a relatively smooth cycling experience. However, be prepared for potential unpaved sections or parts that might be more challenging for certain types of bicycles. It's advisable to use a touring bicycle or a hybrid bike suitable for varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a picturesque journey through the Extremaduran landscape, circling reservoirs and showcasing green areas and forests. Notable highlights include the Historic Center of Brozas, the impressive Castillo de Brozas, the serene Brozas Reservoir, and the beautiful Church of Nuestra Señora de Navas del Madroño.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, covering a distance of 25.4 km with an elevation gain of approximately 156 meters. While not extremely difficult, it does require a good level of physical fitness. Beginners with some cycling experience and a reasonable fitness level should be able to complete it, but it might be challenging for absolute novices.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Vía Pecuaria (11%), Camino de Barrosa (6%), Paseo de los Santos (3%), Camino de Arirrodríguez (3%), Camino de Aliseda (2%), Camino de Villasbuenas (2%), and Calle Valle del Pino (1%).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near the towns of Brozas and Navas del Madroño. You will find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within these towns to refresh yourself before or after your ride. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the tour, as services might be sparse directly on the cycling path between the towns.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Navas del Madroño from Brozas – round trip through the reservoirs?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 25.4 km touring bicycle route is approximately 1 hour and 46 minutes. This duration can vary depending on your cycling pace, stops for sightseeing, and fitness level.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by its scenic round trip through reservoirs, offering beautiful water views. You'll experience the natural beauty of the Extremaduran landscape, which is known for its abundant green areas, forests, and diverse fauna. It's a great opportunity to observe the region's natural environment by bicycle.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de Navas del Madroño
